daily_brief: The deadliest mining disaster in China in 17 years dominated headlines today as a gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in Shanxi province killed at least 90 workers, with nine still missing [BBC World, France 24, Al Jazeera, ABC Australia]. The WHO raised its risk assessment for the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo to 'very high' as suspected cases tripled to 750 with 177 deaths, prompting Oxford University to accelerate vaccine development [The Guardian, BBC World]. Diplomatic efforts to broker a US-Iran peace deal intensified as Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir held talks in Tehran, though Iranian officials cited 'excessive demands' from Washington and US media reported President Trump is 'seriously considering' new strikes [France 24, The Hindu, Financial Times]. Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India for a four-day visit including Quad talks, seeking to reinforce ties following Trump's recent summit with China [France 24, The Hindu]. In Africa, Senegal's President Bassirou Diomaye Faye sacked Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko, deepening political crisis in the debt-laden nation [France 24], while SpaceX successfully launched its massive Starship V3 rocket, deploying 20 dummy satellites before a planned splashdown in the Indian Ocean [BBC World, Le Monde].

top_stories.headline: China Coal Mine Explosion Kills at Least 90 in Deadliest Disaster Since 2009
bullets: Gas explosion occurred at 7:29 PM local time Friday at the Liushenyu coal mine in Shanxi province, with 247 workers underground at the time
At least 90 confirmed dead, nine still missing as of Saturday morning; more than 100 hospitalized and 345 rescue personnel deployed
President Xi Jinping ordered 'all-out efforts' to treat injured and called for thorough investigation; mine executives have been detained
Carbon monoxide levels were found to have 'exceeded limits' prior to the blast; it is China's worst mining disaster since 108 died in Heilongjiang in 2009

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Xi's call for accountability and historical context of China's mining safety improvements

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided similar factual coverage with details on rescue operations

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted poor safety standards and corruption as systemic issues in Chinese mining

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Focused on rescue operations and government response

top_stories.headline: WHO Raises Ebola Risk to 'Very High' in DRC as Cases Triple in One Week
bullets: Suspected cases jumped from 246 to 750 and deaths from 65 to 177 in one week; outbreak involves rare Bundibugyo strain with no approved vaccine or treatment
WHO raised DRC risk assessment from 'high' to 'very high'; regional risk rated 'high' but global risk remains 'low'
Medical facility in Rwampara, Ituri province, was attacked and set on fire Thursday by crowd angry over burial protocols, hampering response efforts
Oxford University developing vaccine using AstraZeneca Covid technology that could be ready for clinical trials in 2-3 months; US travel ban on DRC, Uganda, South Sudan criticized by Africa CDC

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized US public health cuts and USAID dismantling as factors limiting response capacity

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on vaccine development efforts and WHO risk assessment upgrade

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Criticized US travel ban as potentially counterproductive; highlighted 'structural injustice in global health innovation'

top_stories.headline: US-Iran Peace Talks at Critical Juncture as Pakistan Mediates and Trump Weighs New Strikes
bullets: Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir held talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi in Tehran Friday as mediation efforts intensify
Iran accused US of 'excessive demands' while describing gaps in negotiations as 'deep and significant'
President Trump convened senior national security team Friday and is 'seriously considering' new strikes if negotiations fail
Trump changed plans to skip son's wedding to remain in Washington due to 'circumstances pertaining to government,' fueling speculation about sensitive developments

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Presented balanced liveblog coverage noting EU sanctions discussions

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Detailed Pakistani mediation role and Qatari involvement

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Article paywalled but headline emphasized Pakistan's push for deal

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iranian position that stopping war 'on all fronts' is precondition for talks

top_stories.headline: Rubio Begins India Visit to Strengthen Ties After Trump-Xi Summit
bullets: Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India Saturday for four-day, four-city visit including Kolkata, New Delhi, Agra, and Jaipur
Visit begins one week after Trump's 'warm summit' with Chinese leader Xi Jinping; aimed at reassuring key strategic partner
Rubio visited Mother Teresa's Missionaries of Charity headquarters in Kolkata before flying to New Delhi for meetings with PM Modi
Visit includes Quad Foreign Ministers' summit and energy talks amid concerns over Strait of Hormuz closure affecting Indian energy security

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized visit as effort to renew ties with 'usually like-minded partner' after China engagement

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Focused on energy security implications and Quad summit significance

top_stories.headline: Senegal President Sacks Prime Minister Sonko Amid Political Crisis
bullets: President Bassirou Diomaye Faye dismissed Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko and dissolved entire government Friday via decree on state television
Sonko responded on Facebook saying he would 'sleep soundly' and was greeted by hundreds of supporters at his home after midnight
Unusual political situation: Faye owes presidency largely to Sonko, who was barred from running due to defamation conviction
Their Pastef party won 2024 elections promising political reform in the debt-laden West African nation; relationship has soured in recent months

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed context on the unusual political dynamic between the two leaders

additional_stories.headline: SpaceX successfully launches Starship V3, largest rocket in history, deploying 20 dummy satellites before Indian Ocean splashdown
Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of deadly strike on student dormitory in occupied Luhansk; Ukraine says it hit military drone unit
Rubio attempts to reassure NATO allies over confusing US troop deployment announcements after Trump says 5,000 troops will go to Poland
Turkish opposition leader vows to fight court ruling removing him and fellow party leaders in move that strengthens Erdoğan's power
US announces Green Card applicants must now return to home countries to apply, affecting Indian and other immigrants
Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Director of National Intelligence, citing husband's cancer diagnosis
Macron says France must address reparations for its role in slave trade, breaking historic taboo but stopping short of concrete proposals
Poll predicts One Nation would become Australia's federal opposition if election held today, potentially wiping out Coalition
India reeling under severe heat waves with temperatures above 46°C as hospitals report rise in heat-related illnesses
Olympique Lyonnais and Barcelona meet in Women's Champions League final in Oslo, with Lyon seeking record-extending ninth title
